examples.title: Cache Hit/Miss Simulation
examples.description: Simulates cache access and calculates hit ratio
examples.code:
PROGRAM CacheSimulation
BEGIN
  hits ← 0
  misses ← 0
  cache ← empty array of size 64
  
  addresses ← [100, 260, 100, 564, 260, 100, 1024, 564, 100]
  
  FOR each addr IN addresses DO
    blockNum ← addr DIV 16
    lineNum ← blockNum MOD 64
    
    IF cache[lineNum].valid AND cache[lineNum].tag = blockNum THEN
      hits ← hits + 1
      cache[lineNum].lastUsed ← currentTime
    ELSE
      misses ← misses + 1
      cache[lineNum].valid ← TRUE
      cache[lineNum].tag ← blockNum
      cache[lineNum].lastUsed ← currentTime
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  hitRatio ← hits / (hits + misses)
  PRINT "Hit Ratio: " + hitRatio
  PRINT "Average Access Time: " + (hitRatio * 5 + (1-hitRatio) * 105) + " ns"
END
examples.explanation: This simulation demonstrates direct-mapped cache behavior. It calculates hit ratio for a sequence of memory accesses and computes average memory access time assuming 5ns cache and 100ns memory access.

examples.title: LRU Cache Replacement Implementation
examples.description: Implements a simple LRU cache replacement policy using ordered dictionary
examples.explanation: This Python code implements an LRU cache that evicts the least recently used item when capacity is full. The OrderedDict maintains insertion order, and move_to_end() updates the access order.

examples.title: Direct Mapped Cache Lookup
examples.description: C program demonstrating direct-mapped cache address calculation and lookup
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates direct-mapped cache behavior. Given cache size of 64 lines and block size of 16 bytes, it calculates which cache line each address maps to and checks for hit or miss.

examples.title: Write-Back Cache Simulation
examples.description: Java program simulating write-back cache policy with dirty bit tracking
examples.explanation: This Java program simulates write-back policy. When writing, it updates only the cache and marks the line as dirty. Main memory is only updated when that cache line is replaced with new data.
